title: Roasted Smoky Argentinian Beef Skillet
description: A smoky and festive Argentinian dish featuring tender beef and crispy vegetables, cooked to perfection in an iron teppan skillet.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan skillet over medium-high heat to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the kanten-agar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the beef strips to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the olive oil to the preheated skillet and swirl to coat the bottom.
Add the beef strips to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked to desired doneness.
Add the sliced red bell pepper and onion to the skillet and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and lightly charred.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for 1 minute, or until wilted.
Serve the beef and vegetables hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the skillet cool slightly before serving directly from the iron teppan.
